title: MCUQuickStart
sdk_root_tip: All chip SDK packages should be placed under this directory.
err_sdk_root: Please set the SDK root directory.
about_text:
MCUQuickStart v1.2.0

A one-click engineering template generator for STM32/GD32 ARM Cortex-M microcontrollers. Supports Keil MDK, GCC+CMake, FreeRTOS, and RT-Thread Nano.

GitHub: github.com/Majie-xixi/MCUQuickStart

Gitee: gitee.com/mj_yyfddca/mcu_quick_start
help_text:
MCUQuickStart Usage

  1. Set SDK Root: Place all chip firmware packages (STM32/GD32) and optional libraries (FreeRTOS, RT-Thread Nano) in one directory. The tool auto-matches them. .zip/.7z archives in the SDK directory will be auto-extracted.
  2. Select Chip: Choose the family first, then the specific model.
  3. Project Name & Output: The project will be created in a subfolder under the output directory.
  4. Choose Template: Empty (main loop only), LED Blink, or UART Printf.
  5. Optional Libs: Check FreeRTOS or RT-Thread Nano to generate an RTOS-enabled project (mutually exclusive).
  6. HXTAL: Select the actual crystal frequency on your board.
  7. GCC + CMake: Also generate CMakeLists.txt for CLion / VS Code development.
  8. Generate: Open the .uvprojx in Keil MDK, compile and run.
